title: Rough-variance scaling identification theorem
statement: Conjecture: Assume the latent log-volatility process X admits a Volterra representation X_t = X_0 + ∫_0^t K(t,s) dW_s with local kernel singularity K(t,s) ~ c (t-s)^{H-1/2} for H in (0,1/2). Then, as Δ ↓ 0, the increment variance satisfies E[(X_{t+Δ}-X_t)^2] = C_X Δ^{2H} + o(Δ^{2H}), and any realized-variance proxy constructed from sufficiently fine observations for SPY US Equity inherits the same scaling exponent after lower-order noise correction: E[RV_Δ] = a_0 + a_1 Δ^{2H} + o(Δ^{2H}).
assumptions: The volatility driver admits a Volterra representation with local singularity exponent H-1/2.
The observed realized-variance proxy is asymptotically consistent up to lower-order noise distortion.
Empirical scope anchored to SPY US Equity.
failure_conditions: Estimated scaling slope is unstable across windows or frequencies.
Jump-share dominates the short-horizon variance proxy.
No persistent roughness signal appears in the Bloomberg feature panel.
No exact excerpt currently pins down every constant or normalization.
Add explicit symbolic tasks with lhs/rhs identities or qualitative constraints.
next_actions: Add explicit symbolic tasks with lhs/rhs identities or qualitative constraints.
Collect stronger exact excerpts from the book-memory layer.
Add a formal symbolic derivation or export the candidate to Lean for proof work.
source_ref: Starting from the symbolically verified rough-variance scaling theorem for SPY US Equity, propose a sharper theorem linking SPY US Equity and VVIX Index through roughness transmission, variance scaling, and volatility-state dependence. State explicit empirical signatures, symbolic structure, and failure conditions.
